Manufacturing of carbon
materials and chemicals

Himadri’s revenue is primarily
derived from the sale of carbon
materials and chemicals.

Since its inception in 1987, Himadri has grown to become a market leader and prominent player in Speciality Carbon products such as Coal Tar Pitch, Carbon Black, Naphthalene and Refined Naphthalene, SNF and Speciality Oils. Globally, we have been recognized as one of the few companies to offer integrated Speciality Carbon products and have continuously endeavoured to grow our global footprint and mark our presence in a diverse range of industries such as steel, aluminium, graphite, energy storage, tyre, automotive components, plastic, paints, fibre, printing inks, infrastructure development, and many more.
Shandong Dawn Himadri Chemical Industry Limited

Aligned with our core business values, beliefs, and practices, ensuring the safety and well-being of our employees is paramount. We dedicate significant efforts to uphold high standards of Health, Safety, and Environment (HSE) in the workplace, recognizing that any lapses in these areas could have serious consequences.

At Himadri, we maintain strict adherence to health and safety protocols across all our plants and office locations. Our commitment to safety is demonstrated by our ISO 45001:2018 certification. We regularly conduct health and safety training for employees and workers, supplemented by risk assessment, periodic audits and surprise checks to ensure full compliance with all protocols.

A socially responsible corporation recognizes the importance of nurturing strong relationships with all stakeholders, including regulators, investors, suppliers, and customers. At HSCL by placing a high priority on respecting human rights, we show our dedication to building lasting partnerships with stakeholders and safeguarding their rights.

Himadri is dedicated to sustainable development principles, which encompass protecting human rights, respecting individuals' dignity and well-being, and ensuring equal rights for all. As part of this commitment, the Human Rights Policy applies to all employees and investors, while the Supplier Code of Conduct and Sustainable Procurement Policy clearly outlines the expectations for our suppliers.

Recognizing the adverse impact of GHG emissions on the global climate, we acknowledge this as a shared risk. As a corporate entity, we are fully committed to making a positive contribution in this regard.

Himadri diligently monitors its Scope 1 and 2 emissions, striving to enhance energy efficiency. The company is actively evaluating its emissions and has established short-term and long-term plans with defined objectives to improve energy intensity and reduce its carbon footprint.
Additionally, we have initiated evaluating our Scope 3 or value chain emissions which impacts our business activities. Subsequently, we will engage with relevant stakeholders to mitigate Scope 3 emission.
Several processes in our operations require water. Recognizing the significance of this finite resource, we are committed to reducing freshwater usage and optimizing our operations.

Himadri has strategically implemented Zero Liquid Discharge (ZLD) to effectively manage water consumption, treatment, and recycling for internal use. This initiative aims to continuously improve our water consumption intensity.
Going beyond commitments, we are currently expanding our ZLD plant’s capacity to increase water recyclability and reusability.

Product stewardship ensures responsible product lifecycle management. This comprises products designed for durability, recyclability, and safe disposal, reducing waste and resource consumption. Effective product stewardship enhances brand reputation and compliance with meeting the growing demand for sustainable products. It fosters innovation in creating eco-friendly solutions, long-term business sustainability and environmental protection.

Product stewardship involves taking responsibility for a product’s environmental and social impact throughout its life cycle. Himadri can leverage this approach to enhance sustainability, reduce costs, and strengthen market positioning. Our key strategies to further product stewardship are as follows:
Eco-Design & Sustainable Innovation – Develop low-carbon, recyclable, or biodegradable products to meet growing ESG demands.
Circular Economy Integration – Implement closed-loop systems, ensuring product materials are recovered, reused, or repurposed.
Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) Compliance – Align with regulatory requirements by managing end-of-life product disposal efficiently.
Downstream Engagement – Collaborate with customers and recyclers to ensure responsible product use, disposal, and recycling.
Transparency & Certifications – Obtain eco-labels and third-party sustainability certifications to differentiate products in the market.
Customer & Market Advantage – Position sustainable products as premium offerings, attracting environmentally conscious buyers and investors.
By embracing product stewardship, Himadri can enhance brand value, reduce regulatory risks, drive innovation, and capture new revenue streams, ensuring long-term financial and environmental sustainability.

I am pleased to present Himadri’s third Sustainability Report, aligned with GRI standards and driven by our ESG strategy, “Together Towards Tomorrow.” This approach strengthens stakeholder confidence while positioning Himadri as a global leader in sustainability.
At the core of our vision is our commitment to Net Zero by 2050, with SBTi-validated targets including a 25% reduction in Scope 1 & 2 emissions intensity and a 5% reduction in Scope 3 emissions by 2025. Key initiatives—**waste gas-to-power conversion, low-sulfur oils, and circular economy practices—**have already delivered a 36.08% reduction in GHG intensity and 99.56% waste recycling.
Our climate resilience strategy, aligned with TCFD, uses scenario analysis to manage risks and develop low-carbon products like specialty carbon blacks and silicon-graphite anodes.
Safety remains a priority with our Zero-Accident Vision, targeting LTIFR below 1 by 2025 through strict protocols, employee training, and monitoring systems. Similarly, human rights and inclusivity guide our business, in line with the UN Global Compact.
Through sustainable procurement, we assess suppliers on emissions, water use, and labor rights, ensuring alignment with de-carbonization and circular economy goals.
Himadri’s sustainability journey is about innovation, responsibility, and transparency. Together, we are shaping a low-carbon, resilient future for all stakeholders.

At the highest level, the Board of Directors, led by the Chairman cum Managing Director & CEO, is primarily responsible for protecting and enhancing shareholder value through strategic decision-making. 
The Company’s Board provides a strategic guidance across the Company’s operations, ensuring optimal resource utilisation and addressing governance and sustainability. The Company’s decisions are aligned with its vision and mission; strategies prioritise the best interests of all stakeholders. The Company’s Independent Directors lead the Board Committees that implement Board decisions and maintain continuous oversight through thorough reviews of policy and procedure implementation.
Himadri’s ESG governance structure comprises experienced committee members. The Board oversees the ESG subject, including associated risks and opportunities, while the CMD-CEO and other teams are responsible for decisions aligned with objectives. The ESG Committee at the Board level is responsible for overseeing all sustainability matters.
The ESG Council, chaired by the Chief Sustainability Officer, periodically reviews and oversees the implementation of the Company's Sustainability Policies. Under the leadership of the Chief Sustainability Officer, the committee tracks progress, provides updates to the board, and monitors key sustainability KPIs.
The ESG Steering Committee, serving as the highest authority at the unit level, is tasked with the implementation of ESG initiatives. This team collaborates with functional teams, external partners and industry stakeholders to develop roadmaps for sustainability endeavours.

The ESG Committee is a Board Committee responsible for making critical decisions regarding ESG issues, identifying risks, and implementing mitigation strategies.
Additionally, the ESG Council reviews the Company's Business Responsibility and Sustainability Report and recommends it to the Board for adoption. The committee also approves the Company's Sustainability Report. Under the leadership of the Chief Sustainability Officer, the committee tracks progress, provides updates to the board, and monitors key sustainability KPIs.

Himadri is firmly committed to upholding the highest standards of ethics and integrity across all aspects of its operations. Our comprehensive Anti-Bribery and Anti-Corruption Policy reflects this commitment and is fully aligned with national anti-corruption laws. It applies to all employees, including the Board of Directors, Key Managerial Personnel, suppliers, contractors, third-party vendors, and other associated stakeholders.
The policy clearly outlines prohibited practices, robust due diligence protocols for business partners, and strong financial controls designed to prevent fraud and unethical conduct. It reinforces Himadri’s zero-tolerance approach to bribery and corruption, fostering a culture of transparency, accountability, and responsible business practices.
To ensure open and safe communication, a secure and confidential Speak Up / Whistleblower mechanism is in place. Employees and stakeholders are encouraged to report concerns or suspected misconduct without fear of retaliation, through designated channels such as hotlines, email, or web portals.
Himadri places a strong emphasis on awareness and education. Regular training sessions are conducted for employees, business partners, and other stakeholders. All new employees undergo mandatory training on anti-bribery and corruption standards as part of their onboarding.
Audit of Control Procedure:
To maintain the effectiveness of the policy, periodic internal audits and independent third-party assessments are conducted. These evaluations ensure our practices remain aligned with both national and international anti corruption regulations and help identify opportunities for continuous improvement. During FY 2024–25 an independent Anti-Bribery Due Diligence Assessment was conducted by TUV SUD South Asia Pvt Ltd. The assessment covered policy implementation, risk evaluation, thirdparty management, whistleblower protection, and employee awareness across multiple sites. Key findings confirmed that: Himadri maintains a formal, certified ABAC policy with clear accountability structures. Training programs are conducted regularly, covering both new hires and existing employees. Anonymous whistleblower channels are in place and actively used. Periodic risk assessments and due diligence procedures are operational across functions and geographies. The assessment identified opportunities to further enhance third-party training, contract compliance, and targeted risk mitigation. Himadri is committed to acting on these recommendations and embedding ethical governance deeper within its organizational fabric.
Risk Overview:
Bribery and corruption can significantly harm the organisation through various forms, Himadri identified few risks in its operations Legal penalties and financial losses Reputational damage Operational and regulatory disruptions
Misallocation of resources and internal ethical breaches Exposure to blackmail and coercion
There are no reported issues pertaining to corruption and conflicts of interest.

Himadri has instituted robust governance mechanisms to prevent and manage conflicts of interest among Board members, ensuring transparency, accountability, and ethical integrity in decision-making. All members of the Board, including Independent Directors and Senior Management, are bound by a comprehensive Code of Conduct. This code, approved and periodically revised by the Board, provides explicit guidance on expected ethical standards, responsibilities, and protocols for disclosing potential or perceived conflicts of interest.
Key Processes and Safeguards:
1. Disclosure Obligations:
o Directors are required to avoid situations where personal or financial interests may conflict with those of the company.
o In cases where such conflicts are unavoidable,Directors must formally disclose the matter:
For Executives: to their immediate superior.
For Directors: directly to the Board.
2. Board-Level Oversight:
o Disclosed conflicts are addressed and recorded in Board meetings.
o Independent Directors play a critical role in moderating and arbitrating any potential conflict between management and stakeholder interests.
3. Professional Integrity Standards:
o Directors are required to act objectively, ethically, and in the company’s best interest, refraining from actions that could bring personal gain at the company’s expense.
o Abuse of position, exploitation of opportunities arising from their role, and any form of undue influence are strictly prohibited.
4. Independent Directors' Code:
o Independent Directors are required to uphold probity, safeguard stakeholder interests, and report any concerns regarding unethical behavior or potential conflicts.
o They are expected to remain independent in judgment, and report loss of independence, if any, to the Board.
5. Governance Meetings and Evaluations:
o At least one exclusive meeting of Independent Directors is held annually to evaluate:
The performance of the Board and Chairperson.
Flow of information from management.
o Directors' performance evaluations are used as a basis for re-appointment decisions.
6. Training and Awareness:
o Directors are encouraged to undertake regular updates on governance, compliance, and emerging risks, including conflict-of-interest management.
7. Confidentiality and Transparency:
o Directors must maintain confidentiality of internal deliberations and corporate information, disclosing data only under lawful obligation or with proper authorization. Through these structured practices, Himadri ensures
that decisions at the Board level are free from bias, rooted in integrity, and aligned with the principles of
responsible corporate governance. This safeguards not only the company’s operations but also the trust
of investors and other key stakeholders.

Himadri has formalised and widely published its grievance mechanisms, which include a standardised hotline and reporting system. Relevant personnel are trained to ensure that all complaints are reviewed and escalated when necessary. If a complaint is justified, appropriate measures are promptly taken. Employees can report incidents confidentially through a toll-free number, email, or an internal portal. External stakeholders, such as contractors or suppliers, are also encouraged to report noncompliance. A team comprising the Chief Human Resources Officer, Chief Sustainability Officer and Company Secretary handles complaints with sensitivity and impartiality. Employees who report incidents are protected against retaliation, ensuring their safety and confidence in the system. Regular audits and feedback loops help identify areas for improvement and maintain a harassment-free environment.
Whistleblowing concerns can be reported to speakup@himadri.com

Himadri promotes a culture of health and safety while supporting labour and human rights. The Company prioritises the well-being of employees and stakeholders through the implementation of various non-negotiable safety standards and ethical labour practices. This enhances productivity and catalyses sustainable development. As a responsible chemicals Company, Himadri invests in essential elements of health and safety practices. The Company has set ambitious targets and implemented robust measures. Key health and safety priorities were identified through a KPI-driven approach. The Company emphasised the protection of people and property by enforcing health and safety protocols, measure and technologies designed to prevent workplace accidents and ill-health. This approach aligns with Himadri’s Zero Accident/Incident vision. Himadri prioritises employee well-being through health insurance and ESIC coverage; ESG and Safety Committees assess and upgrade safety measures. Risks are countered through controls that are regularly reviewed. Safety measures include advanced fire detection systems, personal protective equipment, compliance with regulatory standards and safe chemical handling procedures. The Company’s sustainability goals align with the UNGC and UNSDGs, reflecting a commitment to minimise workplace injuries, promote well-being and develop sustainable practices. Himadri’s consideration of double materiality reinforces the Company’s commitment to understand and address financial and environmental/social impacts. The Company has mapped processes that affect - and are influenced by - factors within its supply chain from upstream activities to downstream distribution. Double materiality comprises a commitment to health and safety management aligned with international and national standards, extending beyond compliance. By addressing challenges to business continuity, regulatory changes and impacts on people and the planet, the Company balanced profitability with societal and environmental responsibilities. This ensures proactive risk management, operational resilience and sustainable growth. 

OH&S Governance model -
Himadri’s OH&S matters are governed by ESG committee at board. The group, which consists of senior executives and board members, contributes a variety of knowledge in governance, sustainability and industry best practices. The Committee establishes ESG objectives, tracks advancement and guarantees compliance with international norms and laws. It examines and suggests methods to reduce the negative effects on the environment, encourage social responsibility and strengthen governance. The committee makes sure that the company produces sustainable growth and ethical business practices which increase long-term stakeholder value by incorporating ESG into strategy.
At the operational level, the HSE (Health, Safety and Environment) and Sustainability Steering Committee is responsible for planning and executing safety-related actions across business units. This committee, led by unit heads, ensures that the corporate OH&S strategy is effectively implemented at plant and facility levels. By focusing on risk assessments, employee training, emergency preparedness and compliance with global safety standards, this committee acts as a bridge between strategic governance and on-the-ground safety management. The involvement of business unit leaders reinforces accountability and ensures that safety is integrated into everyday business operations.

The HSE Task Force Team operates at the most granular level, ensuring that safety measures are implemented on the ground. This team is responsible for executing safety protocols, defining workplace safety standards and monitoring compliance with regulations. Their role includes hazard identification, conducting safety audits, implementing preventive measures and ensuring that all employees, including contract workers, adhere to safety guidelines. The task force acts as the frontline execution arm of Himadri’s OH&S governance framework, directly influencing the day-to-day safety culture and reinforcing proactive risk mitigation.

Aligning with OH & S management systems: Himadri implemented ISO 45001:2018 for Occupational Health and Safety (OH&S) management systems, providing a comprehensive safety framework. Himadri adheres to the SA 8000:2014 Management Standard, reflecting a deep commitment to social accountability and ethical practices. These protocols guarantee fair treatment, safe working conditions and respect for workers’ rights, including third-party personnel, covering crucial areas such as the prohibition of child labour, health and safety, fair remuneration and a reliable complaint mechanism.

Himadri’s Vision Zero Accident/Incident is grounded in a proactive framework focused on four pillars - Learning, Culture, Commitment and Communication. This vision is dedicated to create a safe, healthy work environment. By implementing comprehensive safety measures, proactive risk management and continuous training, the vision strives to eliminate workplace accidents and incidents. This commitment promotes care, accountability and excellence in health and safety practices, ensuring that every individual returns home safely from work.

The significance of each pillar which drives the zero-incident workplace are:
-	Learning: This pillar focuses on continuous improvement and knowledge enhancement. Trainee employees share their experiences. This results in updated safety practices.
-	Culture: Himadri integrates safety into daily operations, creating a workplace where everyone feels responsible for safety and is encouraged to proactively report hazards. Efforts include the implementation of health and safety standards; introduction of 10 core safety rules; rewards and recognition systems; consequence management practices.
-	Commitment: Himadri’s leadership sets clear expectations, provides necessary resources and leads by example. The employee commitment drives the zero incident goal. Key commitments include: Reducing the lost time injury frequency rate to below 1 by 2025; ensuring 100% of employees are trained in health and safety practices; Establishing ISO 45001:2018 certification across all manufacturing plants by 2025.
-	Communication: Himadri promotes open channels for sharing safety concerns, incidents and best practices. Examples include: Safety alerts; Town Hall and safety committee meetings; Risk assessments and safety audits; Toolbox talks and training sessions. 


Himadri’s journey from traditional safety approaches to Vision Zero Incident/ Accident philosophy focuses on specific targets to prevent accidents, while Vision Zero views safety as an integral journey and part of the business Traditional safety management often comprises programmes led by designated individuals and measures performance through injury benchmarks. In contrast, Vision Zero sees safety as an integral leadership responsibility that is embraced by all employees, viewing incidents as learning opportunities. This approach supports comprehensive safety, integrating safety into business operations and making it a collective responsibility.

Himadri developed and implemented health and safety policies to address critical health and safety elements, aligning with global and national standards prevalent in the chemicals sector.

Health and Safety Policy: Himadri’s Health and Safety Policy promotes workplace safety with a zero-tolerance stance on accidents. The policy identifies and mitigates potential hazards. It emphasises physical and mental well-being. It promotes employee resilience and productivity. It makes safety a shared responsibility. 
Drug and Alcohol Policy: It prohibits the use, possession, or influence of drugs and alcohol on its premises. It enforces this policy through awareness programmes, confidential support resources and a zero-tolerance approach to violations. This promotes resilience among workers, including third-party contractors.
OH&S Standards:  Based on the Policies Himadri established non-negotiable standards across Occupational Health and Safety (OHS) domains, adhering to EN and ISO Standards. Himadri implemented standards for effective workplace stress management.
Risk management: Himadri’s Occupational Safety and Health (OSH) management is grounded in comprehensive risk assessment that identifies potential safety hazards. Himadri’s health and safety risk assessment and mitigation systematically identifies, evaluates and prioritises potential risks. The Company adheres to a risk-based approach in compliance with the ISO 45001:2018 and SA 8000:2014 standards, covering all stakeholders (internal and external). This helps identify workplace risks and implement mitigation measures using systematic tools, reflected in lower Lost Time Injury Frequency Rate. The Company’s risk management is strategic and comprehensive, ensuring that all micro-level activities and relevant sub-activities are covered. 
Himadri follows five risk management steps: 
·	Identification of hazards;
·	Identification of people who might be harmed and how; 
·	Risk evaluation and precaution; 
·	Recording of findings;
·	Review and update.
These collaborative activities involve cross-functional departments, workers’ representatives as well as health and safety professionals, ensuring a holistic approach.

Accident and incident management
The Company’s safety performance over recent years illustrates sustained improvements in health and safety
outcomes. Himadri has achieved measurable progress in reducing workplace incidents and promoting a safer environment. Himadri introduced a tracking sheet for logging injuries and incidents, with corrective and preventive actions shared across sites via a Lesson Learning Sheet (LLS). This helped mitigate similar risks across operations. The Company developed a skilled team of 80 management employees trained in 8D Practical Problem Solving to investigate accidents and implement preventive measures.

Occupational health and safety capacity building
At Himadri, safety practices are marked by continuous improvement, periodic reviews and updated policies. Engaging workers (including third party) has helped percolate safety awareness across levels. During the year under review, the Company launched a new H&S Leadership training module, covering health and safety topics. During the reporting financial year, the Company covered all management category employees. Himadri prioritised a new Permit to- Work standard, training relevant employees and deploying it across divisions, integrating risk management into the system. The Company adopted the 8D (Eight Disciplines) Practical Problem Solving (PPS) method, a structured tool in accident and incident investigations. This method identifies root causes and implements corrective actions. Management employees received 8D-PPS training to investigate incidents and implement solutions. 
During the last financial year, Himadri conducted approximately 25,018 man hours of safety training (classroom sessions, online courses, hands-on drills, interactive workshops and modules on its Utkarsh Learning Management System). This covered employees, workers and third-party contractors, and consumed an average 12 hours per person per year.

Monitoring and auditing for continual improvement:
Himadri’s rigorous approach to health and safety is marked by quarterly audits that cover key ESG areas. These audits are led by qualified internal auditors and members of the Health, Safety and Environment team. Regular evaluations are conducted across the Company’s manufacturing sites, with a focus on Safety Audits, Risk Assessment, Health and Safety Training and KPI-related action plans. In addition to quarterly internal audits, Himadri conducts annual external audits and third-party assessments on key statutory provisions, including IS 14489, which addresses Occupational Health and Safety management system. Moreover we periodically conducts Electrical Safety and Fire Safety Audits (3rd party) following national and international regulations. This layered audit structure helps the Company adhere to high Health and Safety management standards. By integrating internal audits, external reviews and third-party assurance, Himadri has established a comprehensive framework that promotes transparency, accountability and improvement. This approach empowers the Company to communicate advancements towards health and safety Management.

Himadri offers a number of procedures and platforms for employees to formally report work-related risks. The Safety Audit Tool is one such tool that our employees use on the job. Every week, all Himadri employees take part in the workplace audit and report any issues to the relevant department for resolution. We have introduced a QR Code-based reporting system to make it easier for employees to report work-related hazards. Over 32 percent of our employees’ participation increased in FY 24-25 who conducts regular safety audits on a monthly basis. In FY 24-25 we have captured over 9,638 number of safety audit points which is increased from 4,117 Safety Audit Points from the last FY 23-24. Moreover, the closure rate also increased to 86.5% overall which includes various Safety Improvement Opportunities (SIO) to upgrade the Safety Standard.
This led to an overall improvement in worker safety. Employees are involved in this audit program, including line managers, employees, and top management and HSE staff. We will be applying the same rule to temporary and contract workers in the future.

Himadri’s 10 Core Safety rules emphasize personal accountability, adherence to protocols and proactive risk management. These rules include:      
•	Mandatory personal protective equipment (PPE) for all employees
•	Machinery and vehicle operation by authorised personnel 
•	Machine guarding and safety devices
•	Practise Lock, Tag & Try procedure for safe shutdown and de-energisation
•	Permit-to-work for high-risk activities
•	Fall protection for height work
•	Ensuring confined space safety prior to entry
•	Pre-job risk assessments
•	Certified and authorised personnel are permitted for electrical tasks.
•	SWI/SOP for all standard activity

Himadri’s occupational health strategy is structured around comprehensive safety initiatives. This encompasses risk assessment, standardisation of procedures, workplace health promotion, occupational health training, occupational hygiene management, occupational health services, rehabilitation and institutional support for work-related injuries / illnesses and audits (internal and external). The focus is on identifying potential hazards, assessing risks, establishing safety protocols and supporting wellbeing (physical and mental). Himadri emphasises hygiene standards, offers medical support and provides support (rehabilitation and institutional). This holistic approach underlines Himadri’s dedication to sustain a safe and healthy work environment. Risk assessment is key to occupational health at Himadri, ensuring the identification, evaluation and mitigation of workplace health hazards to protect employee safety and well-being. This process begins with identifying potential hazards, assessing their likelihood and severity and implementing control measures to reduce risks. Himadri’s general risk assessment comprises identifying, capturing and recording occupational health risks.

Himadri has implemented a comprehensive standard operating procedure (Framework) to manage industrial noise effectively, ensuring a safe, compliant and comfortable work environment for all employees. Several impactful noise reduction measures have already been implemented. For instance, acoustic hoods have been installed in high-noise areas such as the CETP and RC zones. Through continuous noise monitoring and the adoption of advanced noise mitigation technologies, the Company strives to reduce its environmental impact while supporting a quieter, safer workspace.

Risk assessment is key to occupational health at Himadri, ensuring the identification, evaluation and mitigation of workplace health hazards to protect employee safety and well-being. This process begins with identifying potential hazards, assessing their likelihood and severity and implementing control measures to reduce risks. Himadri’s general risk assessment comprises identifying, capturing and recording occupational health risks. Risks are categorised into high, medium and low levels based on the likelihood of prolonged exposure. High and medium risks are reviewed monthly by the senior management in the ESG Council level to ensure proactive oversight. Himadri prioritises controls, including elimination, reduction, isolation and exposure control. Personal protective equipment is provided at no cost to all employees and contractors, reinforcing the Company’s health-conscious environment.

Standard Activities-These are divided into repetitive and periodic categories. The process for managing standard and non-standard activities enhances the safety focus. For standard activities, which occur either repetitively or periodically, the process begins with risk assessment to identify and mitigate potential hazards. Detailed instructions are developed to ensure safe operations and that employees undergo training and validation to confirm their related competence. The process is reviewed annually. Himadri’s Standardised Work Instructions (SWIs) provide a comprehensive framework that ensures consistency across operations. These SWIs are based on internal standards that are aligned with EN and ISO standards. The key elements of the SWIs include:
·	Safety-Protocols to protect workers (including third party contractors) and prevent accidents.
·	Quality-Guidelines to maintain high standards in product output.
·	Delivery-Timely completion and delivery of processes.
·	Manufacturing Practices-Techniques to establish efficient and effective production methods.
·	Environment-Practices aimed at minimising environmental impact through sustainable approaches.

Non-standard (non-routine) activities-Himadri addresses non-routine work through its Permit to Work (PTW) system. This structured process identifies, communicates, mitigates and controls risks in hazardous areas, non-standard situations and potentially dangerous conditions (shutdowns), enhancing frontline worker safety. In the Company’s 24x7 operations, PTW system is mandatory, typically used in maintenance. Non-standard activities, while frequent, are exceptional and require a customised approach. These activities commence with risk assessment to address specific and unique risks. Before proceeding, PTW ensures safety. Workers are trained and participate in toolbox talks (TBT) to manage associated risks. There are seven main types of work permits: Hot work permits, General work permits, Confined spaces work permits, Electrical work permits, Height work permits, Excavation permits and Radiography work permits. Each work permit is categorised based on the nature of the job and associated hazards, ensuring appropriate safety measures are implemented.

Himadri takes a robust and systematic approach to occupational safety, prioritizing the well-being and security of its employees, contractors, and all stakeholders involved in its operations. The company follows a structured safety framework encompassing multiple interconnected components that ensure a safe, healthy, and resilient workplace.

1. Risk Assessment
The foundation of Himadri’s safety culture begins with proactive risk assessment. Potential hazards are systematically identified and evaluated to implement effective control measures that prevent workplace incidents.


2. Management Standards and Non-Standard Works
The company sets stringent management standards and pays special attention to non-standard or high-risk tasks. This ensures that every work activity, especially those outside routine operations, is carried out with appropriate safety protocols.

3. Workplace Safety
Physical workplace conditions are continuously monitored and improved to eliminate unsafe conditions. Himadri ensures safe infrastructure, proper ergonomics, and adherence to safety guidelines to reduce injury risks.

4. Contractor Safety Management
Safety is extended beyond employees to include all third-party contractors. Himadri ensures contractors comply with internal safety requirements through induction programs, monitoring, and audits.

5. Accident and Incident Management
Any incident, no matter how minor, is thoroughly investigated. Root cause analyses are conducted, and corrective actions are implemented to prevent recurrence and promote a culture of continuous improvement.

6. Emergency Management
Preparedness is key. Himadri conducts regular emergency drills and has response systems in place to manage fires, chemical leaks, and other emergencies, thereby minimizing potential harm to people and property.

7. Engagement of Workforce
A strong safety culture is built on employee involvement. Workers are encouraged to participate in safety committees, report hazards, and contribute suggestions for improving workplace safety.

8. Occupational Safety Capacity Building
Continuous training and capacity-building programs are implemented to enhance employees’ knowledge and competence in safety practices. This includes technical training, behavioural safety programs, and leadership engagement.

9. External/Internal Auditing, Procedure and Review
Regular audits, both internal and external, are conducted to assess compliance with safety standards. These audits help identify gaps and guide the necessary procedural improvements. Himadri implemented internal safety audit standard where the participation percentage increased by 33% than the last financial year.

10. Rewards and Recognition
Himadri recognizes and rewards individuals and teams who contribute significantly to safety improvements. This not only motivates employees but reinforces the value of safe behaviour in the organization.

11. Health and Safety related Digital Initiatives – 

•	Employees at Himadri can report risks, safety issues, and compliance with the daily Tool Box Talk Protocol and the Root Cause Analysis tool for any accident by visiting and interacting with all relevant departments and individuals thanks to the implementation of QR Code-based mobile applications. These also give users access to real-time safety protocol and action status updates, as well as digital safety checklists.

•	Employees can finish the necessary training at their own pace with Himadri's e-learning modules on health and safety subjects, which help them comprehend the leadership's dedication to health and safety as well as the implemented health and safety policy. Additionally, this platform tracks training progress and certifications, offers training schedules and their specifics, and makes sure all staff members are knowledgeable about the most recent safety procedures.

•	Himadri introduces a user friendly digital platform for incident reporting and management. This software allows for efficient logging of incidents, tracking investigations and generating reports, helping to identify trends and implement corrective actions more effectively.

•	We implemented daily Safety Activities for all HSE members at Himadri, which give members a comprehensive overview of all daily health and safety-related activities they participate in and involve department owners in identifying and reducing workplace risks and hazards through collaborative visits and inspections. This system keeps track of daily safety audits, training details, TBT and PTW details, maintenance activities, tanker safety activities, and the necessary responses to anomalies.

12. Act on Opportunities for Continual Improvement
Safety is not static; Himadri continually seeks opportunities for enhancement. Through lessons learned, benchmarking, and technological innovations, the company strives to elevate its safety performance.

Himadri has a robust process for reporting near misses, accident or dangerous occurrences. We ensure all manufacturing plants report on Lost Time Injuries (LTIs), Medically Treated Injuries (MTIs) and First Aid Cases (FACs) to maintain safety focus. Investigations extend to all the Near Misses, First Aid Cases, Dangerous Occurrences and MTIs. First aid cases and low severity DO's are solved using Line Practical Problem Solving tool, while MTI, RSI, LTI, and High Severity DO's cover process-related incidents using the 8D Practical Problem Solving tool. Himadri aims to reach an LTIFR below 1.0, ensuring employee safety and achieving zero accidents.

In this regard the Company encourages an open-door policy. The Company has built a strong mechanism for reporting matters or concerns faced at the workplace. Himadri recognises the importance that business can play in ensuring long term protection of human rights and for this reason the company is dedicated to upholding the human rights of its employees, communities.
The mechanism is outlined below:
1. The employees/ associates can address their complaints or grievances or report instances to the Human Resource department/ Senior Management. No reprisal or retaliatory action is taken against any employee/ associates for raising concerns under this policy.
2. If the situation warrants, a committee is formed to investigate the violations reported. The Committee evaluates the violations reported and ensures that the same are addressed and resolved at the earliest. The Committee also, in consultation with the Senior Management, provides a suitable remedy.
3. The Company periodically undertakes human rights due diligence process for management and oversight/ monitoring of the policy and identifies any shortcomings.
4. The Company also has a Policy on Prevention of Sexual Harassment (POSH), and any such concern can be reported.
Himadri SpeakUp Mechanism – Strengthened Internal Mechanism for Addressing Human Rights Concerns

Himadri’s SpeakUp mechanism is designed to prioritize the highest standards of ethical conduct, workplace safety, and human rights compliance. It ensures that any individual associated with the organization—be it employees, or any stakeholders—can safely report grievances or violations, particularly those impacting dignity, fairness, and wellbeing related to the Human Rights issues.

The mechansim reflects Himadri's commitment to international standards, including:

-	The Universal Declaration of Human Rights
-	The UN Guiding Principles on Business and Human Rights
-	ILO conventions on workplace safety and non-discrimination

Human Rights issues that may be reported -  Himadri SpeakUp supports reporting of any activity that could directly or indirectly violate individual or collective rights. These include:

1.	Unethical Behavior: Harassment, discrimination, bullying, or exploitation
2.	Fraud and Misconduct: Including corruption or misuse of power affecting vulnerable workers
3.	Workplace Safety Risks: Unsafe conditions, lack of protective measures, or forced labor
4.	Gross Negligence: Actions that endanger physical, mental, or emotional health etc


Anonymity and confidentiality are strictly maintained to protect whistleblowers and promote a culture of trust. Mechanism and Workflow is depicted as follows - 

Grievance Identification - Every report is reviewed to determine:

-	Type of issue (e.g., harassment, safety, discrimination)
-	Severity and frequency
-	Potential impact on individuals and the organization

Committee Formation

-	A dedicated investigation committee is formed including internal stakeholders.
-	Where necessary, external experts (legal, compliance, or human rights specialists) are inducted.

Thorough Investigation

-	Detailed fact-finding with evidence collection, interviews, and document review.
-	The process ensures impartiality and non-retaliation.

Decision Making

-	After analyzing all facts, a conclusion is drawn.
-	If the concern is valid, corrective and preventive actions are taken.
-	If unsubstantiated, the rationale is documented and communicated respectfully.



Resolution and Remedy

-	Remedies are implemented in consultation with senior management.

May include:

-	Disciplinary action
-	Policy revisions
-	Victim support
-	Workplace safety improvements
-	Awareness and training sessions

Documentation and Reporting

-	All cases are documented and tracked.
-	Trends are analyzed to strengthen systems and prevent recurrence.

Impact and Assurance

-	Reinforces a safe, inclusive, and rights-respecting workplace
-	Encourages speak-up culture without fear of retaliation
-	Fulfills regulatory, ethical, and social responsibility commitments

The Grievance Redressal Policy clearly outlines that all members involved as a part of the enquiry are entrusted to keep record in a confidential manner. Any employee questioned in respect of the enquiry is bound by confidentiality and is required to keep all paperwork and information exchanged in the process confidential and such practices are followed also while handling and assessing complaints which form a part of the grievance redressal mechanism.
All genuine complaints, in good faith, can be made without fear of reprisals, punishment, intimidation, coercive action, dismissal, or victimization. All actual violations are dealt seriously on the principles of natural justice, confidentiality, sensitivity, nonretaliation, and fairness while addressing the concern.
No significant risk or concerns were identified in our operations.
At Himadri, none of the business processes require any modification or introduction of any new process to address human rights grievances/complaints.

To support our wastewater recycling and Zero Liquid Discharge (ZLD) system, Himadri has established an advanced Water Recovery Plant (WRP)—the final recovery stage of our Effluent Treatment Plant (ETP). This innovative system is designed to maximize water reuse, minimize waste, and significantly reduce our reliance on freshwater resources.

The WRP receives treated wastewater from various sources, including the ETP, DM plant backwash, RO plant reject water, and cooling tower blowdown. These are collected in the WRP feed tank and then directed to a clarifier, where a carefully dosed combination of dolomite, polyelectrolyte, sodium hypochlorite, caustic, and coagulant helps settle Total Suspended Solids. The clarified, TSS-free water is then stored in a dedicated tank.

From here, the water undergoes several advanced filtration processes:
•	It is first passed through a Mixed Gravel Filter (MGF) with Hydrochloric Acid (HCL) dosing to eliminate any residual suspended solids.
•	Next, it enters an Ultrafiltration (UF) unit, which separates permeate and reject water into respective tanks.
•	The permeate water is then treated through a two-stage Reverse Osmosis (RO) system—first stage with five membranes, second stage with two—after pre-treatment using a carbon filter and dosing with HCL, sodium met bisulphite, and anti-scalant.
•	Before reaching the final permeate tank, the water passes through a De-Gasifier unit to remove excess carbon dioxide, ensuring stable pH levels.

The final permeate water, with a daily recovery of 1200 KL, is effectively reused for cooling tower makeup, process requirements, and RO plant feed. Meanwhile, the final concentrate water amounting to 50 KL per day, is sustainability utilized in various places of our manufacturing unit. This integrated system showcases Himadri’s commitment to responsible water management by significantly reducing groundwater consumption and reinforcing our vision for sustainable industrial operations.

Our manufacturing sites present a significant opportunity for enhanced water efficiency. In response, during the reporting year Himadri has started expanding its Zero Liquid Discharge (ZLD) capacity from 450KLD to 1250 KLD ensuring wastewater is treated and reused efficiently, minimizing water withdrawal from external sources. This initiative aligns with our broader water conservation strategy and reinforces our commitment to sustainability. By enhancing ZLD capacity and implementing advanced water recycling systems, we have reduced water usage and operational costs, contributing to resource efficiency. This proactive approach positions Himadri as a leader in sustainable water management, ensuring compliance with regulatory standards while mitigating future water scarcity risks. This strategy not only drives cost savings but also enhances operational resilience against water-related challenges.

At Himadri, we fulfill 90% of our power requirements through our 28MWh capacity power plant, showcasing our dedication to environment preservation. To reduce waste gas and off-gas venting during carbon black production, we’ve implemented an innovative process that recirculates and feeds off-gas into power generation, significantly lowering Greenhouse Gas (GHG) emissions. Our power plant features three boilers that adequately meet the energy demands of our West Bengal unit, with surplus electricity being sold to the local grid. This Financial year we have sold about 82,954.53 MW electricity to the local grid. With our low emission factor in generation of electricity the total emission avoidance is reported at 19,909 tCO2e for FY 2024-25.


•	Himadri Speciality Chemical Limited has replaced the conventional fuel oil heating system with a steam heating system in its new 480 Ton per day Coal Tar distillation project. This transition from a fuel oil heating system to a steam heating system has brought significant improvements in efficiency and effectiveness while lowering associated emission. The fuel required for dehydration has been eliminated, leading to a substantial reduction in GHG emissions. By switching to steam energy, the Company benefits from improved heating performance, potentially lower energy costs, reduced GHG emissions. 

•	Himadri has successfully implemented a high-performance multifunctional fuel additive across its reduction processes, targeting various fuel applications such as furnace oil, carbon black feedstock, light diesel oil and low sulfur heavy stock. This additive has led to significant improvements in fuel efficiency and carbon emission reduction, optimizing production operations.

•	In lieu with our net zero roadmap, Himadri’s greenhouse gas reduction task force helps minimize carbon emissions 
and achieve net-zero. By optimizing processes, adopting cleaner technologies and focusing on Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions, the task force advances sustainability. Ongoing monitoring and reporting ensure compliance, promoting environment responsibility.

•	As Himadri has started integrating environmental impact assessments into the evaluation of each capital expenditure project, recognizing that these decisions significantly influence our long-term sustainability, particularly in terms of GHG emissions. We incorporate an internal carbon pricing for Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions into the payback calculations for investments requiring approval by the CMD-CEO. When evaluating capital expenditures or new projects, the potential CO2 emissions (Scope 1 and Scope 2) are estimated. The internal carbon price is then applied to these emissions to calculate a "carbon cost," which is included in the overall cost-benefit analysis. This ensures that projects with lower carbon emissions are more financially attractive, incentivizing investments in more sustainable options. Himadri implements the shadow pricing mechanism in which we assign a monetary value to each ton of carbon dioxide. Shadow pricing mechanism is a crucial tool to ensure that the environmental implications of long-term investment decisions are fully understood. India being a fast-becoming compliance market for emission. Himadri supports Paris Agreement on Climate Change objectives. And by integrating our actions in reducing our scope 1 emissions we move closer to our sustainability targets of minimizing the scope 1 and scope 2 emission by 30% within 2030. Our Environment Policy and Energy Efficiency Policy provides assurance in our commitment towards a sustainable, pollution-free future.

•	Moreover, we identify that plantation drives offer a valuable opportunity to expand green spaces and promote environmental awareness. Himadri has initiated a significant plantation drive in collaboration with the forest, environment, and climate change department, aiming to enhance the green cover of the village by planting various tree species. The plantation of 6,199 native tree saplings this year is a remarkable achievement. Our plantation drive is designed to achieve enhanced biodiversity, mitigating soil erosion and improving air quality in association with local communities and external subject matter experts. The community’s active involvement in this initiative is truly commendable as it contributes to creating a greener environment. These trees will not only provide shade and clean air for future generations but also enhance the overall health of our ecosystem. Additionally, we regularly organize training for internal stakeholders and awareness campaigns for the external stakeholders on climate change and its effects.

Himadri’s efforts to reduce production waste are guided by a principle: avoid waste. If avoidance is not possible, waste should be recycled or used in energy generation. If recycling or energy generation is not feasible, waste should be disposed safely. The need to establish high-level circular economies that support sustainable economic growth is increasingly critical. Given the planet’s finite resources, rising lifestyle standards and growing resources demand on account of a rising global population, conventional one-way economic models will no longer be able to sustain prosperous lifestyles and cultures going ahead. To stimulate economic activity and move to a decarbonized society, it will be essential to reduce the use of fossil-based plastics while addressing a growing demand for plastics. In this context, Himadri acknowledges the growing importance of reducing plastic consumption and enhancing recycling. As a speciality chemical manufacturing Company, Himadri takes a comprehensive approach to identify risks associated with various types of waste, including production waste, packaging waste, plastics and wood pallets. The Company considers a range of plausible scenarios through consultations with internal and external stakeholders.

Process optimization is central to Himadri. This includes in-plant reprocessing of substance streams and the application of the 3R principle (reduce, reuse, recycle) across operational sites. Where waste cannot be avoided, the focus is on mechanical or thermal reprocessing. At Himadri’s sites, various types of recyclable waste — plastics, glass, paper, food waste and wood—are segregated and dispatched to external recycling firms approved by regional pollution control boards. The Company monitors these firms through audits to ensure their corresponding compliance with statutory provisions.

Himadri adheres to the recommendations of state government waste management authorities related to handling hazardous wastes like effluent sludge, oil-contaminated gloves, jute and soak sand. It employs vendors authorized by the pollution control boards. The Company reintroduced recycled products as inputs following the end-of-life phase of its products. Himadri launched a pilot project to implement a reusable packaging solution, with the potential to eliminate plastics and wood from its packaging.

Besides, the Company strives to achieve its ambitious waste management target (zero waste to landfill). Himadri leverages the benefit of integrated production sites and systems for systematic waste management. By-products from one production process are repurposed as raw materials of other products, widening the value chain. The Company sources raw materials from other sectorial by-products (coal tar and carbon black feedstock). In addition to reprocessing, waste with high calorific value (substitute fuel) is used to produce energy, reducing a reliance on primary fossil fuels and moderating Company’s carbon footprint. Himadri is exploring alternatives to replace virgin materials. 

This includes initiatives to replace fossil-based plastics with alternatives like paper and metal. The Company is progressing to use plant-based plastics as an alternative to fossil-based plastics. At Himadri, all plant waste and process scraps are systematically collected and transported to the main waste yard for segregation into hazardous and non-hazardous categories. Disposal of these wastes is carried out in compliance with Pollution Control Board (PCB) regulations and through PCB approved third-party vendors under the supervision of the Health, Safety and Environment (HSE) function. Himadri maintains regular communication with third-party vendors to monitor the final disposal and ensure proper management of the waste, reaffirming its commitment to environmental responsibility.

Himadri has developed a robust Business Continuity and Disaster Management Plan (BCDMP), which is integrated into the Company’s comprehensive Risk Management Policy. This forward-thinking plan is a testament to Himadri’s unwavering commitment to operational resilience, long-term value creation, and sustainable business growth, even in the face of unforeseen disruptions. Himadri has successfully conducted the Stage 1 audit for ISO 22301:2018, the international standard for Business Continuity Management Systems (BCMS). This audit marks a significant step in Himadri's commitment to ensuring the resilience and continuity of its operations.

The BCDMP serves as a strategic blueprint to ensure uninterrupted operations during natural calamities, industrial emergencies, cyber threats, and other potential disruptions. It reflects Himadri’s focus on protecting stakeholder value, enhancing corporate governance, and aligning with its long-term strategic objectives. The plan outlines procedures to maintain critical business functions, secure employee and community safety, and preserve environmental integrity—minimizing harm to people, infrastructure, biodiversity, and ecosystems.

Himadri’s approach to risk management is proactive and data-driven. All financial and non-financial risks are continuously identified, assessed, and mitigated under the guidance of the Risk Management Committee, a dedicated oversight body that reports directly to the Board. This includes climate-related risks, operational disruptions, supply chain vulnerabilities, and emerging ESG concerns.

To ensure that the BCDMP remains dynamic and effective, regular simulations, risk assessments, and audits are conducted. These evaluations help identify gaps, incorporate learnings, and update response protocols in line with evolving business environments and global best practices. Employees across critical functions are also trained to respond efficiently during emergencies, reinforcing a culture of preparedness and accountability.
Through this integrated and forward-looking risk governance framework, Himadri not only safeguards its assets and people but also ensures that it continues to deliver value to customers and communities—even in times of crisis. This resilience is fundamental to Himadri’s sustainable growth vision and its ability to thrive in an increasingly complex and interconnected world.

This system controls pressure and minimizes vapor emissions, helping to prevent air pollution. It employs nitrogen pressure to control emissions, significantly mitigating odours by condensing light oil vapor into liquid form. By creating an inert atmosphere inside storage tanks, nitrogen blanketing minimizes the presence of oxygen, which in turn reduces the evaporation of VOCs from the liquid surface.

Presently, vent out vapour mixture is rich with nitrogen as it is more volatile than light oil vapour as a result air pollution has reduced from before. As tank is under controlled pressure so that vapour mixture emission has reduced resulting in lowering air pollution.
Waste Heat Recovery : APH, OPH,WHRB & Dryer APH to reduce GHG emissions

The system enhances heat recovery by capturing heat from hot smoke and utilizing it to generate hot air. This recovered heat is then effectively used in the Oil Pre-Heater (OPH)and Captive Power Plant (CPP) boiler combustor.

As a result, Himadri has achieved a total energy usage of1.20 GJ/hr in OPH and 5.45 GJ/hr in CPP. This initiative optimizes the utilization of waste heat, contributing to improved energy efficiency and promoting environmental sustainability.

Himadri's initiative to implement a reduction process for internal waste, such as sludge, represents a significant step forward in sustainable waste management and resource recovery The conversion process adopted by Himadri focuses on transforming the sludge, a by-product typically produced during industrial operations, into a valuable resource. Through this innovative process, the sludge undergoes a transformation, turning it into a usable fuel.

This fuel can then be utilized in auxiliary operations, such as powering certain machine or serving as an alternative energy source for specific industrial activities. 
By harnessing the energy potential of sludge, Himadri is promoting a circular economy model where waste is minimized, and valuable resources are recovered and repurposed.
Reduction of PM2.5 & PM10 –Clean Air Mission

The technology uses pulsed radio waves (Wi-Fi) to accelerate the natural clearance of these microscopic pollutants. This technique coagulates PM2.5 and PM10 through pulsed radio waves which presents several potential benefits for air quality improvement. 
By causing the particulate matter to coagulate and settle, the concentration of harmful particles in the air is reduced.
Air quality typically improves by 30-40% or more, leading to improved health and reduced risk of non-compliance with air pollution norms.
Application of Anthracene Oil in Carbon Black Production

In the context of a circular economy, using Anthracene oil derived from coal tar as a feedstock for carbon black production is an innovative and sustainable approach that significantly contributes to resource efficiency and environmental sustainability.

By redirecting Anthracene oil into the carbon black production process, Himadri is not only creating a valuable industrial product but also closing the loop in the life cycle of coal tar derivatives, ensuring that fewer resources go to waste.
Yield Improvement in Carbon Black Production

Himadri is producing Carbon black by partial combustion of the Anthracene oil, this yield gain process also reduces the extraction of energy transformation as we are converting the higher volume of mass to the finish product, which will help to reduce energy loss and reduce GHG emission also. Yield Gain in CBD: FY-2023-2024 we have achieved the yield gain of 4% due to process excellence process.
The yield gain is a sustainable approach to reduce the raw material consumption to achieve circular economy initiatives as well as to reduce the carbon footprint.
Environmental Impact Reduction : 480 TPD Project –New RC-Coal Tar Division

Himadri has implemented a strategic approach to energy reduction by transitioning from a single-stage cooling system (Old RC) to an advanced double-stage cooling system (New RC). In the previous system, the total heat load was forwarded directly to the cooling tower, leading to higher energy and water consumption. With the New RC system, the total heat load is divided into two streams: one directed towards RO (Reverse Osmosis) water and the other towards the cooling tower.

This division significantly reduces the heat load on the cooling tower, resulting in lower evaporation losses—saving approximately 8 KL of water per day. Additionally, the New RC system improves energy efficiency by converting excess heat into steam, which is forwarded to the LP steam header for reuse.

At Himadri, we follow a structured and responsive approach to ensure that every customer concern is addressed promptly and effectively. When a complaint is raised, regional managers from the Marketing department register it, capturing essential details such as product and grade information, invoice number, nature of the issue, customer or agent details, and any supporting images. The complaint is immediately shared with the Head of Technical Services and the Head of Quality Control. Customers receive an acknowledgment from the Regional Marketing Manager, assuring them that their concern is being acted upon.

Within one working day, the Head of Technical Services forwards the complaint internally to the relevant department heads. These teams collect the necessary data and present their findings in a Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) meeting within two working days. Appropriate corrective steps are then initiated to resolve the issue. Once resolved, the outcome is communicated to the customer by the Marketing Manager. In cases where the complaint is found to be unjustified, the Head of Technical Services and Regional Marketing Manager engage directly with the customer to provide clarification and close the matter amicably.

The complaint closure form is completed by the Head of Technical Services and authorized by the Plant Head. If the resolution identifies any need for process improvements, necessary updates are made to procedures, work instructions, or specifications. All complaints, along with detailed analysis and actions taken, are documented and maintained by the Head of Quality Control—ensuring transparency, accountability, and continuous improvement.

Himadri, as part of its commitment to consumer safety and responsibility, has implemented a series of steps to   educate and inform its consumers about the safe and responsible usage of its products and services.
1.	Clear and detailed product labeling/MSDS: Himadri ensures that all products come with clear & easy to understand labelling that outlines usage instructions, safety precautions and potential hazards.
2.	Comprehensive user manuals and guidelines: These resources often include visual aids and step-by-step instructions.
3.	Customer support and helplines: Himadri has set up dedicated customer support teams and helplines to offer guidance and provide safety related information to consumers.
4.	Online resources and FAQs: Himadri maintain an online resource center that includes frequently asked questions (FAQs), safety tips, troubleshooting guides and product usage videos.
5.	Product training and demonstrations: Himadri offers product demonstrations, either in-person or via virtual platforms, to show consumers the correct way to use its products.
6.	Awareness programs: Himadri sponsors awareness campaigns across various platforms, focusing on the safe and responsible use of its products.
Information related to any risk of disruption/discontinuation of essential services is communicated to consumers through e-mails, phone calls or any such established media.

Himadri places a strong emphasis on clear and informative product displays to ensure consumers have all necessary details for safe and effective use of its products. Himadri’s offerings are designed to be comprehensive, user-friendly and complaint with relevant regulations. This includes not only physical product labelling but also the digital content available on website. The following are the key elements typically included;
1.	Product name and description
2.	Usage instructions
3.	Safety warning and precautions
4.	Environmental impact and disposal instructions
These displays are not only critical for consumer confidence but also play a key role in fostering responsible and informed product usage, which is in line with both ethical business practices and legal requirements.
Himadri's approach to conducting consumer satisfaction surveys is a key component of its strategy for maintaining a high standard of customer service and product quality. By regularly gathering feedback, analyzing results and acting on insights the company ensures that its offerings are aligned with consumer expectations, fostering stronger customer relationships and driving ongoing business success.
